Article Title: MPs ask why govt didn’t inform owners of non-E20 vehicles about mileage loss
Article Text:
MPs questioned why the government did not warn owners of non-E20 vehicles about possible mileage loss.

NEW DELHI: Members of the parliamentary committee on transport Thursday asked top government officials on why no effort was made to inform owners of non-E20 compliant vehicles about potential mileage loss due to use of 20% ethanol blended petrol in these vehicles.TOI has learnt that some members also asked officials whether any economic analysis has been carried out to assess whether E20 fuel has had any gain to individual vehicle owners even as they agreed that the increased ethanol blending in petrol has helped the country to reduce dependence on imported crude.The panel headed by JDU’s Sanjay Jha had called officials from road transport and petroleum ministries, representatives from vehicle testing agencies and oil marketing companies, amid the ongoing controversy over impact of E20 on mileage and engines of old vehicles.Some members asked why there is so much sudden opposition and criticism when govt claims that over 19% ethanol blended fuel has been sold for more than two years.“Some members pointed out that since the controversy has peaked, all departments, vehicle manufacturers, oil companies and test agencies have aligned. They also sought exact mileage loss due to E20 fuel. Road transport ministry officials said mileage loss is between 2 and 6% as opposed to some hearsay claiming to be 25%,” said a person privy to the discussion.One of the participants said that officials pointed out two issues that may have triggered the fresh row were — the BIS notification for higher blended ethanol E22, E25 and E30, and sale of adulterated fuels.
Article Summary: Mps questioned why the government did not warn owners of non-e20 vehicles about possible mileage loss. Some members also asked officials whether any economic analysis has been carried out to assess whether e20 fuel has had any gain to individual vehicle owners.

Article Title: Pvt investment in space sector crosses $618 million, 17 startups given nod for space activities: Minister Jitendra Singh to RS
Article Text:
India's space sector has witnessed unprecedented momentum with private investment crossing $618.5 million

NEW DELHI: India's space sector has witnessed unprecedented momentum following the landmark reforms, with private investment crossing $618.5 million, 105 authorisations issued to private entities, and a robust regulatory framework being strengthened to safeguard strategic and national security interests while promoting greater private participation, Space, science and technology minister Jitendra Singh informed the Rajya Sabha on Thursday.Replying to an unstarred question in the Rajya Sabha, Singh said cumulative private funding has expanded nearly six-fold in the last few years, rising from $100.5 million in 2021-22 to $348.5 million in 2023-24, before reaching $618.5 million by March 31, 2026, reflecting the growing confidence of domestic and global investors in India's rapidly evolving space ecosystem.Singh informed RS that 17 space startups have so far been authorised by IN-SPACe to undertake space activities, reflecting the emergence of a vibrant startup ecosystem that is increasingly contributing to India's space ambitions. Active space startups have increased from just one in 2014 to over 400 today, while private investment has crossed $600 million.Indian private companies have successfully demonstrated launch and orbital capabilities, placed more than 30 satellites into orbit, and flown nearly 45 payloads through platforms such as the PSLV Orbital Experimental Module (POEM), signalling the growing technological depth and commercial maturity of the country's private space sector.The earth observation public-private partnership has brought together leading Indian companies to develop the country's first privately owned Earth observation satellite constellation with a private investment commitment of around Rs 1,200 crore, demonstrating the growing confidence of industry in India's space reforms.Highlighting the govt's unwavering commitment to ensuring that rapid commercial growth is matched by robust national security safeguards, the minister informed the House that every application for private space activities is evaluated under IN-SPACe's norms, guidelines and procedures with due consideration to strategic, security and national interests before authorisation is granted. He further said IN-SPACe is preparing dedicated safety and security guidelines for space activities in India, in consultation with stakeholders, to further strengthen the country's regulatory architecture as private participation continues to expand.
Article Summary: India's space sector has witnessed unprecedented momentum with private investment crossing $618.5 million. Space, science and technology minister jitendra singh informed the rajya sabha on thursday. 17 space startups have so far been authorised by in-space to undertake space activities.

Article Title: Tanul Thakur On The Corporate Cruelty Driving The H-1B Visa Economy
Article Text:
Tanul Thakur’s 'Wild Wild East' Uncovers the Human Cost of the American Dream

Q:

How did this book come about?

A

:

Q: I

t

was

as if your mind

was

exploding

and you had to put it out there to say,

what on earth

are we living side by side with?

A

:

Q: W

hat exactly is the

business

model of a desi

consultancy

?

A

:

Q: How did you persuade the protagonists to talk to you?

Q: T

his H1B

scam

is

kind of a

parallel economy which

seems to have

no closure to it.

A

:

Excerpts:I was an H1B worker in the US from 2011 to 2013. And during my time there as an electrical engineer, I got more than a whiff that there was some scam going on in the H1B visa programme... And this idea really stayed with me, this idea of primarily Indians exploiting Indians in the US. Cut to a few years later, I returned to India to pursue my passion of being a full-time writer and a journalist. And this idea then really thundered back to me in all its different manifestations... I think subconsciously I wanted to write a book that would explore morality, enslavement, identity of modern day Indian elites... The actual story though snowballed into a much bigger and much more significant something...This started off as a very literary project... I wanted to write a "non-fiction novel"... Then these questions started to ping my conscience: why do we not know about this scam? Who benefits from it, apart from the obvious corporate American entities? And then I figured out that this is not just about a few individuals or a few firms.It's essentially like an entire society colluding to both perpetrate and also hide the scam. .. You have these immigration forums, anonymous people with fake monikers talking about how they scam the system. You have visa mills and a bunch of parties coming together to perpetrate something really, really horrific. So the book changed during the process. I changed during the process.Sometimes it felt like a science fiction story... I was constantly taken aback, not just by the brazenness of the scam, but also about how this was hiding in plain sight... On the one hand, you are seeing all these crazy things that are happening, and on the other, there's absolute silence about it in the outer world... There were many heartbreaking things that I found... I was very surprised by the kinds of entities that I used to consider heroes. Say the American media, academia, some politicians — most of them turned out to be villains... Just understanding how it is humanly possible for human beings, in this case three protagonists, to absorb so much of pain, to absorb so much of anguish... on a day to day basis and yet have the courage to solder on. These are the things that really stayed with me.Think of it as a chain of blocks. So essentially you have a client, which is to say like a Fortune 500 firm or a corporate America entity which wants — I mean back in 2010 or something — let's say wanted a Java specialist. So it would ask a big outsourcing giant, which is called a prime vendor, do you have a Java specialist for this kind of a contractual position? If they do, they supply that. If they don't, that's when the desi consultancy model comes in, it would ask a smaller outsourcing or a staffing company. And that's how the chain gets built. You have a client, you have a prime vendor, then you have one or two companies in between. Then you have an H1B employer who is sponsoring the visa for an H1B worker. Now, a decade ago, if a client was paying around $120 per hour for a Java specialist or for an IT specialist with six years of experience, different parties would take a cut and the H1B worker would get somewhere around $25 an hour.And amidst that, there is insane amounts of wage theft. You have your purchase orders being edited, you have no idea how much your employer is getting... There is this thing called benching, wherein you're supposed to pay the H1B worker when he or she doesn't have a project. But that never happens. In fact, you don't just not get paid when you're benched. You have to pay your employers taxes during that period. There is this immense amount of psychological cruelty that's meted by Indian owners of body shops or desi consultancies to Indian H1B workers. This dehumanization exists at varying levels. There's this literal and metaphorical transformation of identity. You are often handed a resume which has seven or eight years of fake experience. Then you get a job through a proxy interview. Even at the client side, if you don't know anything, you have this process of like on job support where you can outsource your job to some other techie in India. So very rampant exploitation almost becomes the language of this industry.First of all, they were themselves so frustrated... Nobody thought their stories were stories. Nobody thought of them as people. So the very fact that I told them that I'm interested in this, that I completely understand your pain, anguish, maybe it had kind of helped that I had done some bit of my own research... I think it was just my persistence. And I think in that persistence, they had seen some bit of my wonder, my innocence, my intellectual curiosity, but also my rage, my very incipient rage. .. They all got eased into the process because I think they had understood that he's not a regular journalist, which is to say that he genuinely cares for us as people... I think being genuinely open, innocent and curious about their lives as people and applying largely the same matrices, but icing it with a copious amount of rage about the systemic abuse itself, I think kind of endeared them to talk to me.What occurred to me around 2020 is that this is a book about the tyranny of capitalism. It's about the tyranny or the dictates of American neoliberalism. And I am essentially just using H1B as a metaphor or as a lens to explore that... One can very easily and glibly dismiss a lot of American workers as racist or bigoted or xenophobic. Some of them could be, sure. But many of them are actually not just these things. They are simply very saddened about their economic and psychological and personal devastation. But that's also one of the biggest corporate cruelties, right? Or it's one of the biggest corporate tricks... A lot of Americans are just blaming Indians, which is insane to me because given the way this H1B system has been abused... they should not be each other's adversaries. They should understand that they are both being pitted against each other... This is something that I want a lot of people to understand, that this is essentially not about immigrants versus citizens, it's about immigrants and citizens versus exploitative corporations and the exploitative American capitalist structure itself.
Article Summary: Tanul thakur was an h1b worker in the us from 2011 to 2013. He says he was shocked by the brazenness of the scam, and how it was hiding in plain sight. The book explores morality, enslavement, identity of modern day indian elites, he says.

Article Title: EU penalises Google €890m for breaking antitrust regulations
Article Text: The European Union (EU) on Thursday hit Google with a fine of ₹890 million after it said the technology behemoth broke digital antitrust regulations by setting up Google Play and its search engine to pull consumers towards its own services and apps to the detriment of competitors. It was the latest major crackdown on Big Tech by Brussels, which has led the world in reining in some of the world's largest companies from Silicon Valley to Beijing.It has done so despite the risk of incurring the wrath of President Donald Trump, who has lashed out at the 27-nation bloc's digital regulations amid a broader campaign against Europe. In the past, Trump has threatened retaliation if American tech companies are penalised.The European Commission, the bloc's executive branch, said that it was acting in the interest of consumers after an investigation of Google. "The best products should succeed because they're better, not because they're owned by the company running the search engine," said Teresa Ribera, the commission's executive vice president for clean, just and competitive transition.Google's president of global affairs Kent Walker blasted the fine as "product degradation driven by a small group of self-serving complainants" that will have a negative impact on European businesses and consumers.He said that the EU's Digital Markets Act forces Google "to strip away real-time search features Europeans love - like instant pricing and direct availability for hotels, flights, and restaurants - and dismantle safety protections on Google Play. "The EU describes the world's seven tech giants - Amazon, Apple, Google parent Alphabet, Meta, Microsoft and TikTok owner ByteDance - as "gatekeepers" that control access for consumers."In the EU, businesses have the right to compete fairly. Gatekeepers have the obligation to ensure a level playing field and consumers the right to choose for cheaper alternative offers," European Commission spokesperson Thomas Regnier said. AP
Article Summary: The european commission said that it was acting in the interest of consumers after an investigation of google. The eu describes the world's seven tech giants - amazon, apple, google parent alphabet, meta, microsoft and tiktok owner bytedance - as "gatekeepers" that control access for consumers.

Article Title: Scientists discovered an underwater city of 60-million fish nests beneath Antarctica's ice shelf
Article Text:
Image: AI Generated

A robot explored beneath Antarctica's ice shelf and confirmed a hidden underwater metropolis

Why these transparent icefish build millions of nests beneath the Antarctic ice

A slightly warmer pocket of ocean makes this extraordinary nursery possible

Why Weddell seals turn this breeding ground into a seasonal hunting ground

Why scientists believe the colony deserves stronger protection

Hidden beneath hundreds of metres of Antarctic ice lies one of the planet's most extraordinary wildlife spectacles. After drilling through the Filchner Ice Shelf, scientists deployed an autonomous underwater robot into the dark waters below, where it revealed what can only be described as an underwater city, a sprawling breeding colony covering an area roughly the size of Orlando, Florida. Spread across the seafloor were an estimated 60 million nests, each carefully built and guarded by transparent, antifreeze-blooded Antarctic icefish. Yet this remarkable nursery is also the setting for a relentless struggle for survival, as hungry Weddell seals transform the breeding season into a deadly feeding ground. The discovery is reshaping scientists' understanding of Antarctica's hidden ecosystems and their importance to the Southern Ocean. It also highlights how much of Antarctica's underwater world remains unexplored, with future expeditions likely to uncover even more hidden ecosystems beneath the continent's vast ice shelves.The vast breeding colony lies beneath the Filchner Ice Shelf in the southern Weddell Sea, one of the most remote and inaccessible marine environments on Earth.The colony was first identified during a 2021 expedition led by Dr Autun Purser of the Alfred Wegener Institute (AWI), Helmholtz Centre for Polar and Marine Research , using the Ocean Floor Observation and Bathymetry System (OFOBS).The team's findings revealed an astonishing concentration of nests extending across approximately 240 square kilometres, an area comparable to the size of Orlando. The researchers estimated that the site contains around 60 million active nests, making it the largest fish breeding colony ever discovered.More recently, AWI scientists returned with LASSIE (Low-Altitude Survey System for Icefish), an autonomous underwater vehicle specifically designed to monitor the colony beneath the ice shelf. Operating continuously for 48 hours, LASSIE confirmed that both the colony's size and nesting density have remained remarkably stable since the original discovery, providing researchers with the first long-term baseline for monitoring this unique ecosystem.The colony belongs to the blackfin icefish (Neopagetopsis ionah), one of Antarctica's most unusual fish species. Icefish are famous for lacking haemoglobin, the red protein that carries oxygen in the blood of almost every other vertebrate. As a result, their blood appears almost transparent. They also produce specialised antifreeze glycoproteins that prevent ice crystals from forming inside their bodies, allowing them to survive in seawater that remains below the normal freezing point of fresh water. These remarkable adaptations have enabled the species to thrive in one of the planet's coldest and most extreme marine environments.Each nest is a circular depression measuring roughly 75 centimetres in diameter, cleared of fine sediment to expose small stones that help protect the eggs. Researchers found that a typical nest contains around 1,700 eggs, while an adult fish, usually the male, stands guard until they hatch. Across the colony, nests occur at an average density of about 0.26 nests per square metre, creating an immense network of breeding sites stretching as far as the cameras could see. The study was led by Dr Autun Purser and an international team of researchers from the Alfred Wegener Institute, the University of Bremen, the Max Planck Institute for Marine Microbiology, the University of Basel and other institutions.aScientists discovered that the breeding colony exists in a remarkably specific part of the seafloor, revealing an unexpected hotspot for Antarctic marine reproduction.Measurements showed that modified Warm Deep Water flowing through the Filchner Trough keeps the seabed beneath the colony up to 2°C warmer than surrounding bottom waters. Although still close to freezing, this subtle temperature difference appears sufficient to create ideal breeding conditions for the icefish.The researchers believe this narrow band of relatively warmer water explains why millions of fish concentrate their nests in one location rather than spreading across the Antarctic seafloor. Without these favourable oceanographic conditions, such an enormous breeding aggregation would probably not exist. The finding also highlights how small environmental differences can shape the distribution and reproductive success of entire marine populations over vast areas.The underwater city does not remain peaceful for long.Using satellite tracking data collected over many years, researchers found that Weddell seals (Leptonychotes weddellii) repeatedly travel to the breeding colony during the nesting season. The dense concentration of adult fish provides an abundant food source, making the area one of the seals' most important feeding grounds.Scientists also documented numerous fish carcasses scattered throughout the colony, demonstrating that while millions of icefish gather to reproduce, many become prey before the breeding season ends. This intense predation helps transfer enormous amounts of energy through the Antarctic food web, supporting predators while recycling nutrients back into the surrounding ecosystem.Rather than representing ecological failure, the seasonal "massacre" is a natural process that links one of Earth's largest fish nurseries with the broader Southern Ocean ecosystem.The discovery has transformed scientific understanding of Antarctic marine ecosystems.According to Dr Autun Purser and colleagues, the breeding colony represents not only the largest known fish nesting site on Earth but also one of Antarctica's most significant concentrations of marine biomass. Because millions of breeding fish gather in a single location each year, researchers argue that the area should receive enhanced conservation protection.The findings have already strengthened proposals for expanding Marine Protected Areas (MPAs) in the Weddell Sea. Long-term monitoring with autonomous systems such as LASSIE will also allow scientists to measure how climate change, ocean warming and shifting sea-ice conditions affect one of the planet's most extraordinary hidden ecosystems.Far beneath Antarctica's frozen surface, where sunlight never reaches and temperatures remain near freezing, an underwater city continues to thrive, its millions of carefully guarded nests reminding scientists that some of Earth's greatest natural wonders remain hidden beneath the ice.
Article Summary: Underwater robot explored beneath antarctica's ice shelf and confirmed a hidden underwater metropolis. The site contains around 60 million active nests, making it the largest fish breeding colony ever discovered. Yet this remarkable nursery is also the setting for a relentless struggle for survival, as hungry weddell seals transform the breeding season into a deadly feeding ground.

Article Title: Boat launches Nirvana Eutopia 2 ANC with dual drivers and 80-hour battery, priced at Rs 4,999
Article Text:
Price and availability

Boat has expanded its flagship Nirvana lineup with the launch of the Nirvana Eutopia 2 ANC, a premium over‑ear headphone designed for users who move seamlessly between music, calls, gaming, travel and work. The new model brings together dual drivers, 45dB Adaptive Hybrid ANC, Hi‑Res Audio, 3D Spatial Audio and an impressive 80‑hour battery life, positioning it as a versatile all‑day companion. Boat says the Eutopia 2 ANC focuses on the features consumers rely on most — sound quality, noise cancellation, comfort, calling clarity and long battery life. “One device now needs to work across music, calls, travel, work and entertainment,” said Gaurav Nayyar, CEO, boAt. “With Nirvana Eutopia 2 ANC, we are building further in the premium segment with a product that is practical, high on performance and aligned with how people use audio devices today.”At the core of the Nirvana Eutopia 2 ANC is a dual‑driver setup engineered to deliver stronger bass, clearer vocals and better separation across tracks. The headphone’s 45dB Adaptive Hybrid ANC adjusts automatically to surrounding noise, keeping the listening experience consistent across commutes, cafés, flights and shared workspaces.For immersive entertainment, the device supports Hi‑Res Audio and 3D Spatial Audio, adding depth and width to music, movies and games. Battery life is another highlight — users get up to 80 hours of playback, and a 10‑minute fast charge delivers up to 12 hours of listening, making it ideal for long travel or workdays.Connectivity includes Bluetooth 5.4 and Google Fast Pair for quick pairing and stable connections. The headphones also integrate with the boAt Hearables App, allowing users to customise EQ settings, controls and listening preferences.Comfort features include Adaptive Fit, premium memory foam ear cushions and a foldable, swivel design for portability. For calls, AI ENx technology reduces ambient noise, while physical buttons offer easy access to playback, volume, voice assistants and ANC modes. The headphones ship with a premium hard carrying case.The boAt Nirvana Eutopia 2 ANC is available in Cosmic Black and Utopian White at a special launch price of Rs 4,999. It can be purchased via the official boAt website and leading e‑commerce platforms.
Article Summary: The boat nirvana eutopia 2 anc is available in cosmic black and utopian white at a special launch price of rs 4,999. The headphone’s 45db adaptive hybrid anc adjusts automatically to surrounding noise. For immersive entertainment, the device supports hi‑res audio and 3d spatial audio, adding depth and width to music, movies and games.

Article Title: Larry Ellison’s Oracle wins Pentagon’s ‘first-ever direct award contract’ for $7 billion
Article Text:
Oracle-Pentagon agreement will initially span for five years

Here’s what Pentagon said in its official announcement

Today, the Department of War (DoW) announced a historic up to 10-year, near $7 billion Enterprise Software Agreement (ESA) with Oracle, further advancing the Arsenal of Freedom. Negotiated by the Department of the Navy, this first-ever, direct award contract with Oracle for the Department's on-premises Oracle usage serves to consolidate licensing and will improve visibility of enterprise usage and spend in support of optimizing the Department's technology budgets. By consolidating fragmented, one-off information technology purchases into a unified, single award contract, the agreement is projected to deliver at least $441 million in savings to U.S. taxpayers over its lifecycle.

"By fundamentally improving how we procure on-premises Oracle capabilities, we are driving at least $441 million in taxpayer savings while rapidly and effectively serving our warfighters," said Honorable Kirsten A. Davies, Chief Information Officer for the Department of War. "This nearly $7 billion agreement with Oracle strengthens our digital ecosystem, supporting our warfighters with secure, scalable technology to dominate current and future missions."

"For the Department of the Navy, this agreement supports faster delivery of secure, scalable software from the shore enterprise to the tactical edge for our Navy and Marine Corps teams," said Barry Tanner, Performing the Duties of the Department of the Navy Chief Information Officer. "Reducing fragmented procurement and advancing enterprise standardization will strengthen interoperability, reduce cybersecurity risk, and help us focus resources on resilient capabilities that can scale, integrate, and endure in contested conditions."

Structured with a five-year base period and a five-year option period, this ESA supports the entire DoW, as well as the Coast Guard and the Intelligence Community.

US Department of War has announced a nearly $7 billion Enterprise Software Agreement (ESA) with Trump supporter Larry Ellison’s Oracle for up to 10 years. The agreement is aimed to consolidate the department’s on-premise software licenses into a single contract. “Negotiated by the Department of the Navy, this first-ever, direct award contract with Oracle for the Department's on-premises Oracle usage serves to consolidate licensing and will improve visibility of enterprise usage and spend in support of optimizing the Department's technology budgets,” the agency said in an announcement.The Oracle-Pentagon agreement will initially span for five years with a further five-year option. "By fundamentally improving how we procure on-premises Oracle capabilities, we are driving at least $441 million in taxpayer savings while rapidly and effectively serving our warfighters," Pentagon ‌Chief ⁠Information Officer Kirsten Davies said.As mentioned above, Oracle founder, Larry Ellison, has a close relationship with the US President Donald Trump. According to a Wall Street Journal report, Ellison has donated more than $45 million to nonprofits and programs backing Trump.
Article Summary: Oracle-pentagon agreement will initially span for five years with a further five-year option. By consolidating fragmented, one-off information technology purchases into a unified, single award contract, the agreement is projected to deliver at least $441 million in savings to u.s. taxpayers.

Article Title: FBI, NSA and CISA issue urgent warning: Iranian hackers hit critical US infrastructure and safety systems
Article Text:
FBI, NSA and CISA issued a joint warning

‘Tampering with safety alarms and emergency shutdowns’



Read the updated warning by FBI, NSA and CISA

The authoring agencies urgently warn U.S. organizations of ongoing Iranian-affiliated cyber targeting of internet-connected operational technology (OT) devices, including programmable logic controllers (PLCs). These actions disrupted PLCs across several U.S. critical infrastructure sectors through malicious project file interactions and manipulation of data on human machine interface (HMI) and supervisory control and data acquisition (SCADA) displays, resulting in operational disruption and financial loss.

Last Update Description

This update adds new guidance on detecting malicious changes in reusable code modules exploited within Rockwell Automation PLC programs. It also expands scope to include observed targeting of

Schneider Electric

, Siemens, and potentially other branded/manufactured PLCs, emphasizing the importance of restricting direct internet access and providing best practices for secure deployment.

Affected Products

Potentially all internet exposed PLCs, including Rockwell Automation/Allen-Bradley, Schneider Electric, Siemens, and other branded/manufactured PLCs.

Key Actions

Install PLCs consistent with manufacturers' guidelines and security best practices.



Remove PLCs from direct internet exposure via secure gateway and firewall; work with IT/OT team members and/or integrators to perform this action.



Query available logs for the provided indicators of compromise (IOCs) and check available logs for suspicious traffic on the ports associated with OT devices, including 44818, 2222, 102, and 502, especially traffic originating from foreign hosting providers.



For Rockwell Automation devices, place the physical mode switch on the controller into run position. If you suspect your organization was targeted, including against other branded PLC devices, contact the authoring agencies and PLC manufacturer for guidance.



US federal cyber agencies have issued an urgent joint warning, claiming that Iranian state-backed hackers are actively breaching and tampering with industrial control systems at US water and energy providers. In an updated security advisory, the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI), the National Security Agency (NSA), the Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA), and the Department of Energy warned that “potentially all internet-exposed” industrial control systems across critical US infrastructure could be vulnerable to attack.According to federal investigators, the Iranian-backed hackers are targeting programmable logic controllers, which are the specialised digital computers that govern physical machinery, valves, and power switches across utility networks.As per the warning, by altering display data and programming logic, the attackers have forced systems into dangerous operational states without alerting human supervisors. CISA and partner agencies are calling on all critical infrastructure operators to immediately audit their operational technology, disconnect industrial control systems from the public internet, and enforce strict multi-factor authentication.
Article Summary: Fbi, nsa and cisa issued a joint warning about iranian-affiliated cyber targeting of internet-connected operational technology (ot) devices. These actions disrupted plcs across several u.s. critical infrastructure sectors through malicious project file interactions and manipulation of data.

Article Title: Microsoft is quietly replacing OpenAI and Anthropic models inside GitHub Copilot, Excel and Outlook, and CEO Satya Nadella just explained the plan
Article Text:
Satya Nadella says Microsoft is now routing tasks inside GitHub Copilot, Excel and Outlook to its own MAI models whenever they match or beat frontier alternatives from OpenAI and Anthropic.

Why Microsoft built its own AI models instead of leaning on OpenAI and Anthropic

What reinforcement learning environments mean for Microsoft's enterprise customers

Microsoft has started routing work inside its own products away from OpenAI and Anthropic, and towards models it built itself. Satya Nadella laid out the reasoning in a long post on X, describing a system where Microsoft's in-house MAI models take over tasks once handled by frontier models from its partners. GitHub Copilot, Excel and Outlook are already showing what he called promising early results. Copilot Chat and PowerPoint are next in line.The logic is cost. Nadella's framing is that software now has a real marginal cost for the first time, which makes the choice of model a business decision rather than a technical default. Frontier models stay in the mix for frontier problems. Everything else gets pushed to something cheaper that performs just as well. Microsoft says its MAI models already beat general-purpose frontier models on many use cases while burning a fraction of the tokens.The MAI family arrived on June 2, when Mustafa Suleyman announced seven new models spanning reasoning, coding, image, voice and transcription. The flagship reasoning model, MAI-Thinking-1, was trained from scratch on what Microsoft calls clean, traceable data—no distillation from other labs.MAI-Code-1-Flash runs 5 billion active parameters and is built specifically for GitHub Copilot and VS Code. Microsoft claims it is comparable to Haiku but cheaper.That cheapness is the entire point. Microsoft's tuned model for Excel matches GPT 5.4 while running up to 10 times more efficiently, according to the company. At Microsoft's scale, across hundreds of millions of Office seats, that gap compounds fast.The mechanism Nadella keeps returning to is the reinforcement learning environment, or RLE. Rather than train a model in isolation and then bolt it onto a product, Microsoft trains models inside the actual product harness, rewarding them for finishing the tasks customers care about. Suleyman calls these training gyms. Microsoft is selling the same capability to enterprises through Foundry under the name Frontier Tuning, letting companies build models on their own workflow data.Nadella was careful about one detail. Models from OpenAI and Anthropic remain part of the orchestration system inside Microsoft products. He also set a test for whether Microsoft is genuinely in control: evaluation scores should keep improving even if any single model is pulled out. Memory, context, tools and skills sit outside the model deliberately, so no one vendor holds the leverage.For Microsoft, which has poured billions into OpenAI, that is a notable position to stake out publicly. The company is describing a template it expects other software firms to copy—use the expensive model where it earns its keep, and route the rest somewhere cheaper you own.
Article Summary: Microsoft has started routing work inside its own products away from openai and anthropic. Satya nadella laid out the reasoning in a long post on x, describing a system where microsoft's in-house mai models take over tasks once handled by frontier models.

Article Title: New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani is hiring software engineers, designers, researchers for his 'tech crew': Here's what the job actually is
Article Text:
NYC mayor wants his ‘tech crew’ to build city software from inside the agency



New York City is betting that in-house tech teams will ship faster and cost less



New York City has opened applications for a new kind of government job. Mayor Zohran Mamdani announced on Monday that the city is recruiting software engineers, product designers, product managers, user researchers, service designers and data experts to staff what he is calling Public Interest Technology Crews—PIT Crews, a pun the mayor committed to by rolling up to the Coney Island go-kart track to make the announcement.The hiring is funded and specific. A City Hall spokesperson confirmed around 30 full-time roles across four crews, supported by $5.24 million in baseline funding in the mayor's new fiscal budget. The listings are live at nyc.gov/pitcrew.A PIT Crew is a mixed team—engineers, designers, product managers, user researchers and data experts—placed alongside staff at a city agency rather than parked in a separate technology department. The crew works with that agency and with New Yorkers to design, build and launch a digital tool, then learn from how people actually use it. The mayor's office describes the timeline as months rather than years, which is the entire premise of the model.Five crews are planned. One handles the Click to Cancel complaint portal, three are attached to Mamdani's affordability and public excellence agenda, and a fifth is being developed with support from The Rockefeller Foundation through the Mayor's Fund to Advance New York City.Zohran Mamdani’s PIT crew’s first assignment is a complaint portal with teethCrew one works with the Department of Consumer and Worker Protection on an online portal where New Yorkers can report companies that make subscriptions near-impossible to cancel. It supports the Click to Cancel rule Mamdani announced last week, which requires businesses to offer the same exit route customers used to sign up. Enforcement begins October 1, and the junk fees ban lands January 1, 2027.The portal is built to do two things at once—give individual New Yorkers a route to recourse, and give City Hall a running database of repeat offenders.Chief Technology Officer and OTI Commissioner Lisa Gelobter framed the roles around a shift in what government technology now is. Every service has a digital surface, she argued, and every confusing step in that surface erodes trust. Her operating instruction for the crews is user first, always, with outcomes weighted over outputs.There is a politics to the hiring as well. NYC-DSA's Tech Action organisers backed the announcement as a choice to build capacity inside government rather than route taxpayer money to consultancies and big tech vendors. BetaNYC's Noel Hidalgo made the cost version of the same argument—in-house teams that deliver faster and cheaper than the outsourced alternative."New Yorkers should not be forced to navigate systems that are confusing, outdated and burdened by bureaucracy," Mamdani said. "The PIT Crew turns that model on its head."Public Interest Technology took shape as a field in the 2010s on a fairly unglamorous premise: government software is usually bad, and fixing it is a staffing and usability problem more than an engineering one. The recruitment page leans into that, pitching the work rather than the salary—the goal, it says, is to make interacting with government dignified and delightful for every New Yorker.
Article Summary: Nyc mayor wants his ‘tech crew’ to build city software from inside the agency. Mayor zohran mamdani announced on monday that the city is recruiting software engineers, product designers, product managers, user researchers, service designers and data experts. Five crews are planned.

Article Title: Nvidia gifts its supercomputer to US military university, donation is first direct use of the company's top-end servers by American military; here's what CEO Jensen Huang said
Article Text:
About DGX GB300 that Nvidia donated to US military university

Nvidia has donated a supercomputer, Nvidia DGX GB300 system with its latest chips, to a nonprofit linked to US military university – Naval Postgraduate School (NPS). The donation marks the first direct use of Nvidia's most advanced servers by the US military. “Our nation depends on our men and women who fight on the front lines. Nothing is more valuable to you than information and insight, and information and insight in a timely way, and to understand its impact and consequence,” Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang said announcing the gift. “I can’t imagine anything more important,” he further added.Huang, who visited Naval Postgraduate School in Monterey, California told the attendees: “Leadership is about being in service of something else. You’re in service of your team. You’re in service of your country. You’re in service of your mission. And all of this technology is just tools to help you”.In a blog post, Nvidia said that the DGX GB300 supercomputer is equipped with Nvidia Mission Control software that gives NPS’s more than 1,500 in-resident students and 600 faculty on-premises access to large-scale AI computing, including model training and inference capability for applications spanning weather prediction, cybersecurity, and disaster resilience and response planning.It’s built based on an Nvidia AI Technology Center on the Monterey campus — a dedicated hub for AI research and graduate instruction, now anchored by the DGX GB300.“As we modernize our technology, we must also modernize how we educate our leaders,” said Admiral Samuel Paparo, commander of the U.S. Pacific Command. “Access to advanced computing capability means NPS students and faculty understand the opportunities and responsibilities that come with these technologies.”Nvidia has partnered with DDN, VAST and Vertiv for hardware and systems integration. DDN, the company said, contributed high-performance data infrastructure to help researchers efficiently access, manage, protect and scale data for demanding AI workloads.VAST Data provided a unified data platform to enable secure data access and management across edge, core and cloud environments. Vertiv provided the racks, cooling and power infrastructure needed to bring the system online, as well as installation sequencing, testing and commissioning support, and fluid management and monitoring.
Article Summary: N nvidia has donated a supercomputer, nvidia dgx gb300 system with its latest chips, to a nonprofit linked to us military university – naval postgraduate school (nps) the donation marks the first direct use of nvidia's most advanced servers by the us military.

Article Title: Netflix unveils ‘VOID’, an AI model that can change a movie plot
Article Text:
Netflix

How Netflix VOID works







Netflix has unveiled an AI model that can alter the scene. While this is nothing new, Netflix’s model not just remove objects from video, it understands what should happen next, essentially altering the causality. Most object-removal technology leaves a gap where something used to be and/ or fills it with a plausible-looking background. VOID goes a step further: after erasing an object from a scene, it calculates how the surrounding environment should realistically respond to its absence, and in the process it adjusts the behaviour of other elements in the frame accordingly.The model, called VOID, is a vision-language system designed to edit video footage with a level of contextual awareness that sets it apart from several existing tools.For example, imagine this scenario: A movie director has just wrapped up a multi-million dollar action sequence where the star drives straight into a fiery, head-on collision. The cars explode, debris scatters, and the scene is a wrap.But suddenly, there is request to change the scene: The character survives and drive off into the sunset instead. This would mean costly reshoots or millions spent on computer-generated graphics.Netflix’s VOID can alter the scene and digitally erase the collision, the second vehicle, the smoke, and the flying debris. It will then generate new, realistic footage of pristine pavement, making it look as though the main character simply drove down an empty, peaceful road.The AI’s ‘magic’ lies in its grasp of real-world physics. Let us understand this by a different example. If a video shows a person jumping into a pool and splashing water onto the deck, VOID can completely erase the person. It will then intelligently alter the water and the wet concrete, leaving behind a perfectly still, undisturbed pool as if nobody had ever jumped in.“To train the model, we generate a new paired dataset of counterfactual object removals using Kubric and HUMOTO, where removing an object requires altering downstream physical interactions. During inference, a vision-language model identifies regions of the scene affected by the removed object. These regions are then used to guide a video diffusion model that generates physically consistent counterfactual outcomes,” Netflix said.“Experiments on both synthetic and real data show that our approach better preserves consistent scene dynamics after object removal compared to prior video object removal methods,” the company added.Netflix isn’t keeping this powerful tool locked behind studio doors, and made the VOID model available to the public on the AI platform Hugging Face, allowing anyone to download and use it.While there are already several video-altering tools on the market, such as Runway, DiffuEraser, and ProPainter, the Netflix team claims VOID is vastly superior, as per The Register.
Article Summary: Netflix has unveiled an ai model that can alter the scene. While this is nothing new, netflix’s model not just remove objects from video, it understands what should happen next. Most object-removal technology leaves a gap where something used to be and/ or fills it with a plausible-looking background.

Article Title: AI data centers are causing 'stress' not just to tech companies, but also private insurers
Article Text:
Massive investments, growing risks



Unique challenges for data centre insurance companies





AI data centers are reportedly putting new pressure on the insurance industry. According to a CNBC report, experts warn that the rapid growth of these projects is becoming a “stress test” not just for tech companies, but also for private insurers and lenders. Consulting firm McKinsey estimates that global spending on data centers could reach $7 trillion by 2030. As costs rise, tech giants are increasingly relying on private equity, private credit and debt to fund these projects. The scale of investments is creating challenges for insurers.“When you put $10 to $20 billion plus in a single location, it creates capacity issues in the marketplace,” said Tom Harper, data center leader at insurance broker Gallagher as quoted in the CNBC report.He added that the sector has been a “real stress test” over the last few years, as insurers struggle to provide coverage for such large and complex projects. While these facilities are seen as high-quality assets, Harper said insuring them has become difficult due to their size and risk concentration.Private data center deals have crossed $10 billion regularly, with some reaching as high as $40 billion. Companies including Nvidia, Microsoft and BlackRock have already taken part in large investment deals.Rajat Rana, partner at Quinn Emanuel Urquhart & Sullivan, described the scale as unprecedented. “We’re talking about trillions of dollars, and almost going back to the same cycle where there’s almost no transparency about the financing structures — the scale is astronomical,” he said. He added that the boom in AI data centers could be “the largest peacetime investment project in human history.”Experts say the way these projects are financed is adding another layer of risk. Much of the funding is happening through complex and less transparent debt structures. Rana warned that this trend feels like “deja vu,” referring to past financial cycles. He said the lack of transparency could create risks for investors, insurers and pension funds.As stated in the CNBC report, data centers offer unique challenges due to their mix of real estate and advanced technology. Insurers are now designing custom policies to manage these risks. However, challenges remain. Large facilities are often located in areas prone to extreme weather, and they store expensive equipment, sometimes even before installation.Another key concern is the mismatch between the lifespan of data centers and the hardware they use. While data centers can last decades, GPUs — the chips powering AI — typically last around seven years. Rana described this as a “GPU debt treadmill,” saying, “This is almost like a treadmill that these AI data centers are running on.”He explained that companies may need to keep raising debt to upgrade infrastructure as technology evolves.
Article Summary: Private data center deals have crossed $10 billion regularly, with some reaching as high as $40 billion. Mckinsey estimates that global spending on data centers could reach $7 trillion by 2030. Tech giants are increasingly relying on private equity, private credit and debt to fund these projects.

Article Title: Clavicular Viral Controversy: "Trans women are.....": Clavicular’s viral moment with trans women sparks fresh conversation on internet culture
Article Text:
A viral clip featuring Clavicular and trans women is gaining attention for its calm and honest tone. The conversation explores how online language and trends move across communities. Instead of conflict, the exchange highlights shared influence and mutual awareness. Viewers are engaging with its thoughtful nature, as it reflects how internet culture evolves through collaboration rather than ownership.

Clavicular and trans women share a surprising realization about online trends







The video of Clavicular, a short-video producer, is going around the Internet, yet not the reason why. There is no yelling or over-the-top drama. It is a mere, everyday talk that seems almost novel nowadays, in the age of internet, which is overflowing with content. The scene is easy-going, the tone of the narrative is straightforward, and the characters appear more concerned with conversation than combat.The message that is being addressed is the flow of the conversation. It discusses internet culture, the way people communicate with one another and the place of trends. Rather than bringing out a debate, the encounter towards curiosity. It quietly brings attention to something that a lot of people who use the internet notice but don't often say out loud. People in different communities often have a bigger impact on each other than they think.The conversation begins with a confident and lighthearted comment from one of the trans women present. She says, "You know that trans women are like the OG lip masters, right? We are very talented in the program. Like, I do the peptides, I'm on Zeta." Her tone carries a mix of pride and humor, setting a relaxed vibe at the table.Clavicular responds in a way that keeps the mood steady but adds a different angle.He says, "That is funny because you guys were using a lot of the words in like the South Korean subreddits in like 2014." It’s not confrontational. If anything, it feels like an observation. He points to how certain words and ideas may have traveled across different online spaces over time.Another voice joins in and brings the moment full circle. "Guys, we're like the OGs. I saw a couple of posts about that. We're like copying each other." That line hits in a simple but honest way. It reflects something familiar. Online culture is rarely created in isolation. It grows through sharing, borrowing, and sometimes even unknowingly repeating.That's what makes the clip so powerful. It doesn't want to make a point or win an argument. It only shows a group of people talking, listening, and figuring something out together.This moment feels different in a space where viral content often leads to fights. It reminds people that conversations can be calm, thoughtful, and still important. And sometimes, that honest silence is what makes it work.
Article Summary: A viral clip featuring clavicular and trans women is gaining attention for its calm and honest tone. The conversation explores how online language and trends move across communities. Viewers are engaging with its thoughtful nature, as it reflects how internet culture evolves through collaboration rather than ownership.

Article Title: China launches Gravity-1 rocket from sea, places nine satellites into orbit
Article Text:
A Gravity-1 carrier rocket carrying nine satellites blasts off from waters off the coast of Shanghai, east China, July 22, 2026. (Xinhua)

Nine satellites to support Earth observation, AI and disaster management

China on Wednesday successfully launched its Gravity-1 commercial rocket from the East China Sea, placing nine satellites into their designated orbits and carrying out one payload experiment. The mission marked the first far-sea launch by a privately developed commercial rocket, demonstrating China's growing capability in long-range maritime launch operations.Developed by ORIENSPACE, the 30-metre, 405-tonne Gravity-1 lifted off at 10:54 am from a launch vessel operated by the Oriental Aerospace Port in Shandong province. The launch was conducted under the direction of the Taiyuan Satellite Launch Center.Officials said the mission successfully validated the rocket's ability to operate under complex offshore conditions, remain launch-ready for extended periods at sea, and support cross-sea launch operations.The payload included nine satellites—Dongpo-13, Dongpo-14, Dongpo-17 to Dongpo-20, Xiguang-2 01, Tianyi-49 and Lilac-3. The six Dongpo-series satellites, comprising optical and synthetic aperture radar (SAR) payloads, will join an existing constellation to improve remote-sensing coverage for applications such as land mapping, disaster response, agriculture, forestry and water management.Meanwhile, Xiguang-2 01 and Tianyi-49 are designed for AI-powered Earth observation, offering services in mineral exploration, environmental monitoring, urban planning and agriculture. Lilac-3 will test next-generation satellite platform and attitude-control technologies through in-orbit demonstrations.Gravity-1 is the world's first medium-lift launch vehicle with all-solid-propellant strap-on boosters, capable of carrying 6.5 tonnes to low-Earth orbit and 4.2 tonnes to a 500-km sun-synchronous orbit. Chinese officials said the mission also marked another step in expanding the country's coordinated sea-launch network and improving operational flexibility for future commercial space missions.
Article Summary: China successfully launched its gravity-1 commercial rocket from the east china sea on wednesday. The mission marked the first far-sea launch by a privately developed commercial rocket. The payload included nine satellites to support earth observation, ai and disaster management.

Article Title: Quote of the day by John Cena: "When I hear people boo, that just makes me want to go out there and…"- an inspiring lesson on criticism, resilience and turning negativity into motivation
Article Text:
John Cena

Quote of the day by John Cena

"When I hear people boo, that just makes me want to go out there and work harder"

What is the meaning behind John Cena’s quote

Criticism is part of being visible

Your reaction matters more than the criticism itself

Why hard work tends to answer critics better than arguing does

Not all criticism feels the same, and Cena has said so himself

Resilience is built slowly, through repeated difficulty

Confidence built on effort tends to last longer than confidence built on praise

You cannot please everyone, and trying usually backfires

Other famous quotes by John Cena

"Never give up."

"You can't see me."

"Be loyal to those who are loyal to you. And respect everyone, even your enemies and competition."

"Always do your best. Never give up."

Why this quote is important

Almost everyone gets criticised at some point, whether at work, at school, or just from strangers online. Some of it helps you improve. Some of it just stings. John Cena , the wrestler and actor, found a simple way to deal with the part that stings. "When I hear people boo, that just makes me want to go out there and work harder," he said. Instead of letting the noise get to him, he decided to use it as fuel. It sounds like a small mindset shift, but it changes almost everything about how a setback actually feels once it happens.The first part of the quote is honest about something real. Booing means people disapprove, and in wrestling that reaction is loud, public and impossible to ignore. Most performers would find that discouraging. Cena's response flips it completely. Rather than letting the boos knock his confidence, he treats them as a reason to try even harder the next time he steps into the ring.That is really the whole lesson packed into one short sentence. Negative reactions do not have to be a reason to give up. They can become a reason to keep going, and to go further than you would have without them.The more well-known someone becomes, the more opinions they attract, and not all of those opinions will be kind. Athletes get criticised after a loss. Business leaders get criticised for tough decisions. Even scientists get their research questioned by other experts. Nobody who becomes successful manages to avoid criticism entirely, and John Cena's own career is a clear example of that pattern. Even at the height of his fame, plenty of fans booed him loudly, night after night, in packed arenas.Nobody can control what other people think or say. What a person can control is how they respond to it. One person might hear criticism and decide they are simply not good enough. Another might hear the exact same words and treat them as motivation. The criticism does not change. The response does, and that response is really the only part anyone actually has power over.Trying to argue with every critic rarely changes anyone's mind. Consistent effort usually says more than words ever could. An athlete gets better through training. A student improves through studying. A professional grows by learning new skills. Cena's approach is to put his energy into improving rather than getting distracted trying to win over people who were never going to be won over anyway.It is worth being honest here, since Cena's own view of criticism is a little more layered than the single quote suggests on its own. He has said he treats any loud reaction in the arena, whether cheers or boos, as a kind of compliment, since it means people are actually paying attention and engaged with what he is doing. What he has described very differently is online criticism, calling some of the harsher comments he sees on the internet baffling, since that kind of negativity feels different from the noise of a live crowd.He has also drawn a clear line when criticism has crossed into something that felt more personal. On The Pat McAfee Show in 2025, he told fans directly that being repeatedly mocked about his hairline had actually pushed him into getting a hair transplant, describing the years of chanting as straightforward bullying that he did not enjoy. That honesty matters. It shows that even someone famous for embracing boos still draws a line between criticism that motivates him and criticism that simply hurts.Nobody becomes resilient during the easy periods of life. It tends to build up gradually, through setbacks handled one at a time. Every piece of criticism absorbed without giving up becomes practice at managing that feeling. Every disappointment survived becomes proof, to yourself, that you can survive the next one too. That slow accumulation of small recoveries is really what builds genuine confidence over time, not a single dramatic moment of toughness.Praise feels good, but it depends entirely on other people choosing to give it. Effort belongs to you alone. Someone who knows they have actually put the work in tends to feel steady in that knowledge regardless of what anyone else says about them. Cena's own quote points in that exact direction, suggesting his satisfaction comes from knowing he gave a genuine effort, not from whether the crowd happened to applaud that particular night.It is natural to want everyone to like what you do, but that is rarely realistic. Different people want different things, and no single approach satisfies every single person watching. Rather than chasing universal approval, which is close to impossible, it makes more sense to focus on maintaining your own standards and continuing to improve regardless of who is cheering and who is not.Criticism travels faster and reaches further than it ever has, thanks to social media. A single post can attract praise and harsh criticism within minutes of being shared. That makes learning to handle negative reactions more useful than ever, whether the criticism comes from a packed arena or a comment section. John Cena's approach offers one practical way through it. Rather than getting pulled down by every negative voice, put the energy into the thing you actually control, your own effort. It will not stop every critic from booing. It will keep you improving regardless of whether they do.
Article Summary: John cena's quote of the day: "when i hear people boo, that just makes me want to go out there and work harder" the wrestler and actor found a simple way to deal with the part that stings. He treats any loud reaction in the arena as a kind of compliment. It is natural to want everyone to like what you do, but that is rarely realistic.

Article Title: After two years of banning bee-harming pesticides, France allows their return, facing backlash from environmentalists
Article Text:
Why France is allowing the pesticides again

Which pesticides are returning



Why environmentalists oppose the move

Farmers say they need the exemptions

Political controversy deepens

Safeguards included in the law

The wider debate over farming and biodiversity

France has approved the temporary return of two controversial pesticides that had been banned nationally for the past two years, reigniting a fierce debate between agricultural competitiveness and environmental protection. Lawmakers backed an emergency farm bill that allows limited use of the insecticides under strict conditions, arguing that French farmers need the chemicals to remain competitive with rivals across the European Union. Environmental groups, however, say the move weakens France's long-standing commitment to protecting bees and biodiversity. The decision has triggered political tensions, public criticism and renewed concerns over the potential impact of the pesticides on pollinators, ecosystems and human health.The French National Assembly approved an emergency agricultural bill that permits temporary exemptions for two insecticides: acetamiprid and flupyradifurone. Both products remain authorised at the European Union level, but France had imposed stricter national restrictions.Supporters of the legislation argue that French growers have struggled to compete because farmers in neighbouring EU countries can still use the same pesticides.The government says the exemptions will help protect crops from destructive insect pests while keeping domestic agriculture competitive.The law allows limited use of acetamiprid, a neonicotinoid insecticide, mainly for hazelnut production. Flupyradifurone, which belongs to a different class of insecticides, may be authorised for crops including sugar beet, apples and cherries.The exemptions are not automatic. Each application must first receive approval from France's food and health safety agency, ANSES, and will be granted only when no effective alternatives are available.Environmental organisations have strongly criticised the legislation, arguing that the chemicals pose risks to pollinating insects such as bees, which play a vital role in food production and ecosystem health.Critics also warn that easing pesticide restrictions could undermine France's reputation as one of Europe's leaders in environmental protection. Campaigners fear the temporary exemptions could set a precedent for weakening future pesticide regulations.French farming groups welcomed the decision, saying they have been placed at a competitive disadvantage for years. They argue that banning products still available elsewhere in the EU increases production costs, reduces crop yields and makes it harder for French agriculture to compete in international markets.According to supporters, the temporary exemptions are intended to bridge the gap until safer and equally effective alternatives become widely available.The legislation has exposed divisions within the French government and Parliament. While supporters describe it as a pragmatic response to farmers' concerns, opponents see it as a step backwards for environmental policy.The controversy intensified after Environment Minister Monique Barbut resigned following the bill's passage, highlighting the political sensitivity surrounding the government's decision.The exemptions are designed to be temporary rather than permanent. They can be granted for a maximum of three years and only after scientific evaluation confirms that no viable alternatives exist for specific crops.Officials say the restrictions aim to balance agricultural needs with environmental protection, although critics remain unconvinced that the safeguards are sufficient.France's decision shows a broader challenge facing many countries: how to maintain food production while reducing the environmental impact of farming. Governments across Europe continue to face pressure from farmers seeking greater flexibility and from environmental groups demanding stronger protections for pollinators and biodiversity.
Article Summary: Lawmakers backed an emergency farm bill that allows limited use of the insecticides under strict conditions. The decision has triggered political tensions, public criticism and renewed concerns over the potential impact of the pesticides on pollinators, ecosystems and human health.

Article Title: Quote of the day by Xenophanes: 'If horses or oxen or lions had hands… horses would paint the forms of gods like horses' and a universal truth about human psychology
Article Text:
Quote of the day by Xenophanes (Photo: AI)

World of Homer and Hesiod

What does Xenophanes's famous quote teach us?

A lesson on human psychology

Xenophanes of Colophon (c. 570 – c. 478 BCE) was an ancient Greek philosopher, poet, and social critic. Born in Ionia (modern-day Turkey), he spent most of his long life wandering across the Greek world as a traveling poet after his hometown was conquered by the Persians.Unlike many thinkers of his time who focused purely on physical science, trying to determine whether the world was made of water, air, or fire, Xenophanes was fascinated by human nature, culture, and religious belief. He was one of the earliest Western thinkers to criticize popular religion, social norms, and the blind acceptance of tradition.“If horses or oxen or lions had hands and could draw with their hands and accomplish such works as men do, horses would draw the forms of the gods like horses, and oxen like oxen, and they would make their bodies such as the shape which they each have," he said, unveiling a tenet of human psychology so ahead of its time. He explained that everyone imagines everything like them.For the average Greek, religion came straight from the epic poems of Homer (The Iliad and The Odyssey) and Hesiod (Theogony). These stories depicted gods like Zeus, Hera, Poseidon, and Ares. But these gods didn’t just look like tall, attractive humans; they also acted like them. They threw tantrums, cheated on their spouses, lied, fought out of jealousy, and held petty grudges.Xenophanes found this absurd. He argued that Homer and Hesiod had attributed to the gods "everything that is a shame and a reproach among men: stealing, committing adultery, and deceiving each other."This led Xenophanes to ask a deeper question: Where do our ideas of the divine actually come from? Are we discovering the truth, or are we just making things up in our own image?Xenophanes realized that humans are inherently ego-driven. We assume that we are the standard for the universe. Because we have two arms, two legs, and walk upright, we assume God or the divine must look like a glorified version of us.By bringing horses, oxen, and lions into the equation, Xenophanes strips away human arrogance. He shows that our mental picture of God isn't based on divine revelation; it’s based on self-love. If a horse thinks a horse is the peak of physical creation, of course its god would have hooves and a mane.Just after the horse-and-oxen passage, Xenophanes made another famous observation: "The Ethiopians say that their gods are snub-nosed and black; the Thracians that theirs are blue-eyed and red-haired."He was pointing out that every culture paints God to look like its own people. A northern European tribe sees a fair-skinned god; an African culture sees a dark-skinned god. Xenophanes realized that religion is often just a mirror reflecting the culture that created it.Xenophanes wasn't an atheist. He didn't use this argument to say God doesn't exist. Instead, he was arguing for humility. He believed that there is a divine reality, but that human beings are too limited to see it clearly. When we try to picture God, we can't help but use our limited human experience as a template. By making God look and act like us, we reduce something vast and ultimate into something small, familiar, and comfortable.While Xenophanes' quote was about God, what he revealed is applicable everywhere. We constantly judge other people, animals, and nature through the narrow lens of human experience. Xenophanes was reminding us that the world does not revolve around human beings. Before we claim to understand ultimate truths, we need to step back and ask ourselves: Am I seeing reality as it truly is, or am I just a horse painting a picture of a giant horse?
Article Summary: Xenophanes of colophon was an ancient greek philosopher, poet, and social critic. He was one of the earliest western thinkers to criticize popular religion, social norms, and the blind acceptance of tradition. Xenophanes realized that religion is often just a mirror reflecting the culture that created it.
